=== Quick Fix Inventory ===

[D1] (11:14-11:33) TS377047: This expression can be rewritten in the reusable function form `Effect.fnUntraced(function*<T>(value) { ... })`. effect(effectFnOpportunity)
  Fix 0: "Disable effectFnOpportunity for this line"
  Fix 1: "Disable effectFnOpportunity for entire file"
  Fix 2: "Convert to Effect.fnUntraced"
  Fix 3: "Convert to Effect.fn (no span)"
  Fix 4: "Convert to Effect.fn(\"arrowWithTypeParams\")"

[D2] (12:10-14:5) TS377017: This Effect.gen contains a single return statement. effect(unnecessaryEffectGen)
  Fix 0: "Disable unnecessaryEffectGen for this line"
  Fix 1: "Disable unnecessaryEffectGen for entire file"
  Fix 2: "Remove the Effect.gen, and keep the body"

[D3] (17:14-17:46) TS377047: This expression can be rewritten in the reusable function form `Effect.fnUntraced(function*<T>(value) { ... })`. effect(effectFnOpportunity)
  Fix 0: "Disable effectFnOpportunity for this line"
  Fix 1: "Disable effectFnOpportunity for entire file"
  Fix 2: "Convert to Effect.fnUntraced"
  Fix 3: "Convert to Effect.fn (no span)"
  Fix 4: "Convert to Effect.fn(\"functionExpressionWithTypeParams\")"

[D4] (18:10-20:5) TS377017: This Effect.gen contains a single return statement. effect(unnecessaryEffectGen)
  Fix 0: "Disable unnecessaryEffectGen for this line"
  Fix 1: "Disable unnecessaryEffectGen for entire file"
  Fix 2: "Remove the Effect.gen, and keep the body"

[D5] (23:17-23:50) TS377047: This expression can be rewritten in the reusable function form `Effect.fnUntraced(function*<T>(value) { ... })`. effect(effectFnOpportunity)
  Fix 0: "Disable effectFnOpportunity for this line"
  Fix 1: "Disable effectFnOpportunity for entire file"

  Fix 0: "Disable unnecessaryEffectGen for this line"
  Fix 1: "Disable unnecessaryEffectGen for entire file"
  Fix 2: "Remove the Effect.gen, and keep the body"

  Fix 0: "Disable effectFnOpportunity for this line"
  Fix 1: "Disable effectFnOpportunity for entire file"
  Fix 2: "Convert to Effect.fn (no span)"
  Fix 3: "Convert to Effect.fn(\"arrowWithTypeParamsNoGen\")"

  (no quick fixes)

=== Quick Fix Application Results ===

=== [D1] Fix 0: "Disable effectFnOpportunity for this line" ===
skipped by default

=== [D1] Fix 1: "Disable effectFnOpportunity for entire file" ===
skipped by default

=== [D1] Fix 2: "Convert to Effect.fnUntraced" ===

--- file:///.src/effectFnOpportunity_typeParams.ts ---
import * as Effect from "effect/Effect"

// Note: Known formatting divergences from upstream (TypeScript-Go printer artifacts):
// - Semicolons added to body statements inside replaced functions (e.g. `const a = 1;` vs `const a = 1`)
// - Space before parens in zero-parameter function expressions (`function ()` vs `function()`)
// - Trailing semicolons on replacement statements (`});` vs `})`)

// The diagnostic should trigger for these cases. We should check that
// the quick fixes have kept the generic type arguments.

export const arrowWithTypeParams = Effect.fnUntraced(function* <T>(value: T) {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(value);
});

export const functionExpressionWithTypeParams = function<T>(value: T) {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(value)
  })
}

export function functionDeclarationWithTypeParams<T>(value: T) {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(value)
  })
}

// Regular functions with type parameters returning Effect (no Effect.gen)
// These have >5 statements so they should trigger the diagnostic

export const arrowWithTypeParamsNoGen = <T>(value: T) => {
  const _a = 1
  const _b = 2
  const _c = 3
  const _d = 4
  const _e = 5
  return Effect.succeed(value)
}

export const functionExpressionWithTypeParamsNoGen = function<T>(value: T) {
  const _a = 1
  const _b = 2
  const _c = 3
  const _d = 4
  const _e = 5
  return Effect.succeed(value)
}

export function functionDeclarationWithTypeParamsNoGen<T>(value: T) {
  const _a = 1
  const _b = 2
  const _c = 3
  const _d = 4
  const _e = 5
  return Effect.succeed(value)
}


=== [D1] Fix 3: "Convert to Effect.fn (no span)" ===

--- file:///.src/effectFnOpportunity_typeParams.ts ---
import * as Effect from "effect/Effect"

// Note: Known formatting divergences from upstream (TypeScript-Go printer artifacts):
// - Semicolons added to body statements inside replaced functions (e.g. `const a = 1;` vs `const a = 1`)
// - Space before parens in zero-parameter function expressions (`function ()` vs `function()`)
// - Trailing semicolons on replacement statements (`});` vs `})`)

// The diagnostic should trigger for these cases. We should check that
// the quick fixes have kept the generic type arguments.

export const arrowWithTypeParams = Effect.fn(function* <T>(value: T) {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(value);
});

export const functionExpressionWithTypeParams = function<T>(value: T) {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(value)
  })
}

export function functionDeclarationWithTypeParams<T>(value: T) {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(value)
  })
}

// Regular functions with type parameters returning Effect (no Effect.gen)
// These have >5 statements so they should trigger the diagnostic

export const arrowWithTypeParamsNoGen = <T>(value: T) => {
  const _a = 1
  const _b = 2
  const _c = 3
  const _d = 4
  const _e = 5
  return Effect.succeed(value)
}

export const functionExpressionWithTypeParamsNoGen = function<T>(value: T) {
  const _a = 1
  const _b = 2
  const _c = 3
  const _d = 4
  const _e = 5
  return Effect.succeed(value)
}

export function functionDeclarationWithTypeParamsNoGen<T>(value: T) {
  const _a = 1
  const _b = 2
  const _c = 3
  const _d = 4
  const _e = 5
  return Effect.succeed(value)
}


=== [D1] Fix 4: "Convert to Effect.fn(\"arrowWithTypeParams\")" ===

--- file:///.src/effectFnOpportunity_typeParams.ts ---
import * as Effect from "effect/Effect"

// Note: Known formatting divergences from upstream (TypeScript-Go printer artifacts):
// - Semicolons added to body statements inside replaced functions (e.g. `const a = 1;` vs `const a = 1`)
// - Space before parens in zero-parameter function expressions (`function ()` vs `function()`)
// - Trailing semicolons on replacement statements (`});` vs `})`)

// The diagnostic should trigger for these cases. We should check that
// the quick fixes have kept the generic type arguments.

export const arrowWithTypeParams = Effect.fn("arrowWithTypeParams")(function* <T>(value: T) {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(value);
});

export const functionExpressionWithTypeParams = function<T>(value: T) {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(value)
  })
}

export function functionDeclarationWithTypeParams<T>(value: T) {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(value)
  })
}

// Regular functions with type parameters returning Effect (no Effect.gen)
// These have >5 statements so they should trigger the diagnostic

export const arrowWithTypeParamsNoGen = <T>(value: T) => {
  const _a = 1
  const _b = 2
  const _c = 3
  const _d = 4
  const _e = 5
  return Effect.succeed(value)
}

export const functionExpressionWithTypeParamsNoGen = function<T>(value: T) {
  const _a = 1
  const _b = 2
  const _c = 3
  const _d = 4
  const _e = 5
  return Effect.succeed(value)
}

export function functionDeclarationWithTypeParamsNoGen<T>(value: T) {
  const _a = 1
  const _b = 2
  const _c = 3
  const _d = 4
  const _e = 5
  return Effect.succeed(value)
}


=== [D2] Fix 0: "Disable unnecessaryEffectGen for this line" ===
skipped by default

=== [D2] Fix 1: "Disable unnecessaryEffectGen for entire file" ===
skipped by default

=== [D2] Fix 2: "Remove the Effect.gen, and keep the body" ===

--- file:///.src/effectFnOpportunity_typeParams.ts ---
import * as Effect from "effect/Effect"

// Note: Known formatting divergences from upstream (TypeScript-Go printer artifacts):
// - Semicolons added to body statements inside replaced functions (e.g. `const a = 1;` vs `const a = 1`)
// - Space before parens in zero-parameter function expressions (`function ()` vs `function()`)
// - Trailing semicolons on replacement statements (`});` vs `})`)

// The diagnostic should trigger for these cases. We should check that
// the quick fixes have kept the generic type arguments.

export const arrowWithTypeParams = <T>(value: T) => {
  return Effect.succeed(value)
}

export const functionExpressionWithTypeParams = function<T>(value: T) {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(value)
  })
}

export function functionDeclarationWithTypeParams<T>(value: T) {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(value)
  })
}

// Regular functions with type parameters returning Effect (no Effect.gen)
// These have >5 statements so they should trigger the diagnostic

export const arrowWithTypeParamsNoGen = <T>(value: T) => {
  const _a = 1
  const _b = 2
  const _c = 3
  const _d = 4
  const _e = 5
  return Effect.succeed(value)
}

export const functionExpressionWithTypeParamsNoGen = function<T>(value: T) {
  const _a = 1
  const _b = 2
  const _c = 3
  const _d = 4
  const _e = 5
  return Effect.succeed(value)
}

export function functionDeclarationWithTypeParamsNoGen<T>(value: T) {
  const _a = 1
  const _b = 2
  const _c = 3
  const _d = 4
  const _e = 5
  return Effect.succeed(value)
}


=== [D3] Fix 0: "Disable effectFnOpportunity for this line" ===
skipped by default

=== [D3] Fix 1: "Disable effectFnOpportunity for entire file" ===
skipped by default

=== [D3] Fix 2: "Convert to Effect.fnUntraced" ===

--- file:///.src/effectFnOpportunity_typeParams.ts ---
import * as Effect from "effect/Effect"

// Note: Known formatting divergences from upstream (TypeScript-Go printer artifacts):
// - Semicolons added to body statements inside replaced functions (e.g. `const a = 1;` vs `const a = 1`)
// - Space before parens in zero-parameter function expressions (`function ()` vs `function()`)
// - Trailing semicolons on replacement statements (`});` vs `})`)

// The diagnostic should trigger for these cases. We should check that
// the quick fixes have kept the generic type arguments.

export const arrowWithTypeParams = <T>(value: T) => {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(value)
  })
}

export const functionExpressionWithTypeParams = Effect.fnUntraced(function* <T>(value: T) {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(value);
});

export function functionDeclarationWithTypeParams<T>(value: T) {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(value)
  })
}

// Regular functions with type parameters returning Effect (no Effect.gen)
// These have >5 statements so they should trigger the diagnostic

export const arrowWithTypeParamsNoGen = <T>(value: T) => {
  const _a = 1
  const _b = 2
  const _c = 3
  const _d = 4
  const _e = 5
  return Effect.succeed(value)
}

export const functionExpressionWithTypeParamsNoGen = function<T>(value: T) {
  const _a = 1
  const _b = 2
  const _c = 3
  const _d = 4
  const _e = 5
  return Effect.succeed(value)
}

export function functionDeclarationWithTypeParamsNoGen<T>(value: T) {
  const _a = 1
  const _b = 2
  const _c = 3
  const _d = 4
  const _e = 5
  return Effect.succeed(value)
}


=== [D3] Fix 3: "Convert to Effect.fn (no span)" ===

--- file:///.src/effectFnOpportunity_typeParams.ts ---
import * as Effect from "effect/Effect"

// Note: Known formatting divergences from upstream (TypeScript-Go printer artifacts):
// - Semicolons added to body statements inside replaced functions (e.g. `const a = 1;` vs `const a = 1`)
// - Space before parens in zero-parameter function expressions (`function ()` vs `function()`)
// - Trailing semicolons on replacement statements (`});` vs `})`)

// The diagnostic should trigger for these cases. We should check that
// the quick fixes have kept the generic type arguments.

export const arrowWithTypeParams = <T>(value: T) => {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(value)
  })
}

export const functionExpressionWithTypeParams = Effect.fn(function* <T>(value: T) {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(value);
});

export function functionDeclarationWithTypeParams<T>(value: T) {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(value)
  })
}

// Regular functions with type parameters returning Effect (no Effect.gen)
// These have >5 statements so they should trigger the diagnostic

export const arrowWithTypeParamsNoGen = <T>(value: T) => {
  const _a = 1
  const _b = 2
  const _c = 3
  const _d = 4
  const _e = 5
  return Effect.succeed(value)
}

export const functionExpressionWithTypeParamsNoGen = function<T>(value: T) {
  const _a = 1
  const _b = 2
  const _c = 3
  const _d = 4
  const _e = 5
  return Effect.succeed(value)
}

export function functionDeclarationWithTypeParamsNoGen<T>(value: T) {
  const _a = 1
  const _b = 2
  const _c = 3
  const _d = 4
  const _e = 5
  return Effect.succeed(value)
}


=== [D3] Fix 4: "Convert to Effect.fn(\"functionExpressionWithTypeParams\")" ===

--- file:///.src/effectFnOpportunity_typeParams.ts ---
import * as Effect from "effect/Effect"

// Note: Known formatting divergences from upstream (TypeScript-Go printer artifacts):
// - Semicolons added to body statements inside replaced functions (e.g. `const a = 1;` vs `const a = 1`)
// - Space before parens in zero-parameter function expressions (`function ()` vs `function()`)
// - Trailing semicolons on replacement statements (`});` vs `})`)

// The diagnostic should trigger for these cases. We should check that
// the quick fixes have kept the generic type arguments.

export const arrowWithTypeParams = <T>(value: T) => {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(value)
  })
}

export const functionExpressionWithTypeParams = Effect.fn("functionExpressionWithTypeParams")(function* <T>(value: T) {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(value);
});

export function functionDeclarationWithTypeParams<T>(value: T) {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(value)
  })
}

// Regular functions with type parameters returning Effect (no Effect.gen)
// These have >5 statements so they should trigger the diagnostic

export const arrowWithTypeParamsNoGen = <T>(value: T) => {
  const _a = 1
  const _b = 2
  const _c = 3
  const _d = 4
  const _e = 5
  return Effect.succeed(value)
}

export const functionExpressionWithTypeParamsNoGen = function<T>(value: T) {
  const _a = 1
  const _b = 2
  const _c = 3
  const _d = 4
  const _e = 5
  return Effect.succeed(value)
}

export function functionDeclarationWithTypeParamsNoGen<T>(value: T) {
  const _a = 1
  const _b = 2
  const _c = 3
  const _d = 4
  const _e = 5
  return Effect.succeed(value)
}


=== [D4] Fix 0: "Disable unnecessaryEffectGen for this line" ===
skipped by default

=== [D4] Fix 1: "Disable unnecessaryEffectGen for entire file" ===
skipped by default

=== [D4] Fix 2: "Remove the Effect.gen, and keep the body" ===

--- file:///.src/effectFnOpportunity_typeParams.ts ---
import * as Effect from "effect/Effect"

// Note: Known formatting divergences from upstream (TypeScript-Go printer artifacts):
// - Semicolons added to body statements inside replaced functions (e.g. `const a = 1;` vs `const a = 1`)
// - Space before parens in zero-parameter function expressions (`function ()` vs `function()`)
// - Trailing semicolons on replacement statements (`});` vs `})`)

// The diagnostic should trigger for these cases. We should check that
// the quick fixes have kept the generic type arguments.

export const arrowWithTypeParams = <T>(value: T) => {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(value)
  })
}

export const functionExpressionWithTypeParams = function<T>(value: T) {
  return Effect.succeed(value)
}

export function functionDeclarationWithTypeParams<T>(value: T) {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(value)
  })
}

// Regular functions with type parameters returning Effect (no Effect.gen)
// These have >5 statements so they should trigger the diagnostic

export const arrowWithTypeParamsNoGen = <T>(value: T) => {
  const _a = 1
  const _b = 2
  const _c = 3
  const _d = 4
  const _e = 5
  return Effect.succeed(value)
}

export const functionExpressionWithTypeParamsNoGen = function<T>(value: T) {
  const _a = 1
  const _b = 2
  const _c = 3
  const _d = 4
  const _e = 5
  return Effect.succeed(value)
}

export function functionDeclarationWithTypeParamsNoGen<T>(value: T) {
  const _a = 1
  const _b = 2
  const _c = 3
  const _d = 4
  const _e = 5
  return Effect.succeed(value)
}


=== [D5] Fix 0: "Disable effectFnOpportunity for this line" ===
skipped by default

=== [D5] Fix 1: "Disable effectFnOpportunity for entire file" ===
skipped by default

=== [D5] Fix 2: "Convert to Effect.fnUntraced" ===

--- file:///.src/effectFnOpportunity_typeParams.ts ---
import * as Effect from "effect/Effect"

// Note: Known formatting divergences from upstream (TypeScript-Go printer artifacts):
// - Semicolons added to body statements inside replaced functions (e.g. `const a = 1;` vs `const a = 1`)
// - Space before parens in zero-parameter function expressions (`function ()` vs `function()`)
// - Trailing semicolons on replacement statements (`});` vs `})`)

// The diagnostic should trigger for these cases. We should check that
// the quick fixes have kept the generic type arguments.

export const arrowWithTypeParams = <T>(value: T) => {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(value)
  })
}

export const functionExpressionWithTypeParams = function<T>(value: T) {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(value)
  })
}

export const functionDeclarationWithTypeParams = Effect.fnUntraced(function* <T>(value: T) {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(value);
});

// Regular functions with type parameters returning Effect (no Effect.gen)
// These have >5 statements so they should trigger the diagnostic

export const arrowWithTypeParamsNoGen = <T>(value: T) => {
  const _a = 1
  const _b = 2
  const _c = 3
  const _d = 4
  const _e = 5
  return Effect.succeed(value)
}

export const functionExpressionWithTypeParamsNoGen = function<T>(value: T) {
  const _a = 1
  const _b = 2
  const _c = 3
  const _d = 4
  const _e = 5
  return Effect.succeed(value)
}

export function functionDeclarationWithTypeParamsNoGen<T>(value: T) {
  const _a = 1
  const _b = 2
  const _c = 3
  const _d = 4
  const _e = 5
  return Effect.succeed(value)
}


=== [D5] Fix 3: "Convert to Effect.fn (no span)" ===

--- file:///.src/effectFnOpportunity_typeParams.ts ---
import * as Effect from "effect/Effect"

// Note: Known formatting divergences from upstream (TypeScript-Go printer artifacts):
// - Semicolons added to body statements inside replaced functions (e.g. `const a = 1;` vs `const a = 1`)
// - Space before parens in zero-parameter function expressions (`function ()` vs `function()`)
// - Trailing semicolons on replacement statements (`});` vs `})`)

// The diagnostic should trigger for these cases. We should check that
// the quick fixes have kept the generic type arguments.

export const arrowWithTypeParams = <T>(value: T) => {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(value)
  })
}

export const functionExpressionWithTypeParams = function<T>(value: T) {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(value)
  })
}

export const functionDeclarationWithTypeParams = Effect.fn(function* <T>(value: T) {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(value);
});

// Regular functions with type parameters returning Effect (no Effect.gen)
// These have >5 statements so they should trigger the diagnostic

export const arrowWithTypeParamsNoGen = <T>(value: T) => {
  const _a = 1
  const _b = 2
  const _c = 3
  const _d = 4
  const _e = 5
  return Effect.succeed(value)
}

export const functionExpressionWithTypeParamsNoGen = function<T>(value: T) {
  const _a = 1
  const _b = 2
  const _c = 3
  const _d = 4
  const _e = 5
  return Effect.succeed(value)
}

export function functionDeclarationWithTypeParamsNoGen<T>(value: T) {
  const _a = 1
  const _b = 2
  const _c = 3
  const _d = 4
  const _e = 5
  return Effect.succeed(value)
}


=== [D5] Fix 4: "Convert to Effect.fn(\"functionDeclarationWithTypeParams\")" ===

--- file:///.src/effectFnOpportunity_typeParams.ts ---
import * as Effect from "effect/Effect"

// Note: Known formatting divergences from upstream (TypeScript-Go printer artifacts):
// - Semicolons added to body statements inside replaced functions (e.g. `const a = 1;` vs `const a = 1`)
// - Space before parens in zero-parameter function expressions (`function ()` vs `function()`)
// - Trailing semicolons on replacement statements (`});` vs `})`)

// The diagnostic should trigger for these cases. We should check that
// the quick fixes have kept the generic type arguments.

export const arrowWithTypeParams = <T>(value: T) => {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(value)
  })
}

export const functionExpressionWithTypeParams = function<T>(value: T) {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(value)
  })
}

export const functionDeclarationWithTypeParams = Effect.fn("functionDeclarationWithTypeParams")(function* <T>(value: T) {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(value);
});

// Regular functions with type parameters returning Effect (no Effect.gen)
// These have >5 statements so they should trigger the diagnostic

export const arrowWithTypeParamsNoGen = <T>(value: T) => {
  const _a = 1
  const _b = 2
  const _c = 3
  const _d = 4
  const _e = 5
  return Effect.succeed(value)
}

export const functionExpressionWithTypeParamsNoGen = function<T>(value: T) {
  const _a = 1
  const _b = 2
  const _c = 3
  const _d = 4
  const _e = 5
  return Effect.succeed(value)
}

export function functionDeclarationWithTypeParamsNoGen<T>(value: T) {
  const _a = 1
  const _b = 2
  const _c = 3
  const _d = 4
  const _e = 5
  return Effect.succeed(value)
}


=== [D6] Fix 0: "Disable unnecessaryEffectGen for this line" ===
skipped by default

=== [D6] Fix 1: "Disable unnecessaryEffectGen for entire file" ===
skipped by default

=== [D6] Fix 2: "Remove the Effect.gen, and keep the body" ===

--- file:///.src/effectFnOpportunity_typeParams.ts ---
import * as Effect from "effect/Effect"

// Note: Known formatting divergences from upstream (TypeScript-Go printer artifacts):
// - Semicolons added to body statements inside replaced functions (e.g. `const a = 1;` vs `const a = 1`)
// - Space before parens in zero-parameter function expressions (`function ()` vs `function()`)
// - Trailing semicolons on replacement statements (`});` vs `})`)

// The diagnostic should trigger for these cases. We should check that
// the quick fixes have kept the generic type arguments.

export const arrowWithTypeParams = <T>(value: T) => {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(value)
  })
}

export const functionExpressionWithTypeParams = function<T>(value: T) {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(value)
  })
}

export function functionDeclarationWithTypeParams<T>(value: T) {
  return Effect.succeed(value)
}

// Regular functions with type parameters returning Effect (no Effect.gen)
// These have >5 statements so they should trigger the diagnostic

export const arrowWithTypeParamsNoGen = <T>(value: T) => {
  const _a = 1
  const _b = 2
  const _c = 3
  const _d = 4
  const _e = 5
  return Effect.succeed(value)
}

export const functionExpressionWithTypeParamsNoGen = function<T>(value: T) {
  const _a = 1
  const _b = 2
  const _c = 3
  const _d = 4
  const _e = 5
  return Effect.succeed(value)
}

export function functionDeclarationWithTypeParamsNoGen<T>(value: T) {
  const _a = 1
  const _b = 2
  const _c = 3
  const _d = 4
  const _e = 5
  return Effect.succeed(value)
}


=== [D7] Fix 0: "Disable effectFnOpportunity for this line" ===
skipped by default

=== [D7] Fix 1: "Disable effectFnOpportunity for entire file" ===
skipped by default

=== [D7] Fix 2: "Convert to Effect.fn (no span)" ===

--- file:///.src/effectFnOpportunity_typeParams.ts ---
import * as Effect from "effect/Effect"

// Note: Known formatting divergences from upstream (TypeScript-Go printer artifacts):
// - Semicolons added to body statements inside replaced functions (e.g. `const a = 1;` vs `const a = 1`)
// - Space before parens in zero-parameter function expressions (`function ()` vs `function()`)
// - Trailing semicolons on replacement statements (`});` vs `})`)

// The diagnostic should trigger for these cases. We should check that
// the quick fixes have kept the generic type arguments.

export const arrowWithTypeParams = <T>(value: T) => {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(value)
  })
}

export const functionExpressionWithTypeParams = function<T>(value: T) {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(value)
  })
}

export function functionDeclarationWithTypeParams<T>(value: T) {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(value)
  })
}

// Regular functions with type parameters returning Effect (no Effect.gen)
// These have >5 statements so they should trigger the diagnostic

export const arrowWithTypeParamsNoGen = Effect.fn(function <T>(value: T) {
    const _a = 1;
    const _b = 2;
    const _c = 3;
    const _d = 4;
    const _e = 5;
    return Effect.succeed(value);
});

export const functionExpressionWithTypeParamsNoGen = function<T>(value: T) {
  const _a = 1
  const _b = 2
  const _c = 3
  const _d = 4
  const _e = 5
  return Effect.succeed(value)
}

export function functionDeclarationWithTypeParamsNoGen<T>(value: T) {
  const _a = 1
  const _b = 2
  const _c = 3
  const _d = 4
  const _e = 5
  return Effect.succeed(value)
}


=== [D7] Fix 3: "Convert to Effect.fn(\"arrowWithTypeParamsNoGen\")" ===

--- file:///.src/effectFnOpportunity_typeParams.ts ---
import * as Effect from "effect/Effect"

// Note: Known formatting divergences from upstream (TypeScript-Go printer artifacts):
// - Semicolons added to body statements inside replaced functions (e.g. `const a = 1;` vs `const a = 1`)
// - Space before parens in zero-parameter function expressions (`function ()` vs `function()`)
// - Trailing semicolons on replacement statements (`});` vs `})`)

// The diagnostic should trigger for these cases. We should check that
// the quick fixes have kept the generic type arguments.

export const arrowWithTypeParams = <T>(value: T) => {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(value)
  })
}

export const functionExpressionWithTypeParams = function<T>(value: T) {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(value)
  })
}

export function functionDeclarationWithTypeParams<T>(value: T) {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(value)
  })
}

// Regular functions with type parameters returning Effect (no Effect.gen)
// These have >5 statements so they should trigger the diagnostic

export const arrowWithTypeParamsNoGen = Effect.fn("arrowWithTypeParamsNoGen")(function <T>(value: T) {
    const _a = 1;
    const _b = 2;
    const _c = 3;
    const _d = 4;
    const _e = 5;
    return Effect.succeed(value);
});

export const functionExpressionWithTypeParamsNoGen = function<T>(value: T) {
  const _a = 1
  const _b = 2
  const _c = 3
  const _d = 4
  const _e = 5
  return Effect.succeed(value)
}

export function functionDeclarationWithTypeParamsNoGen<T>(value: T) {
  const _a = 1
  const _b = 2
  const _c = 3
  const _d = 4
  const _e = 5
  return Effect.succeed(value)
}

=== [D13] Fix 1: "Disable effectFnOpportunity for entire file" ===

=== [D13] Fix 2: "Convert to Effect.fn (no span)" ===

--- file:///.src/effectFnOpportunity_typeParams.ts ---
import * as Effect from "effect/Effect"

// Note: Known formatting divergences from upstream (TypeScript-Go printer artifacts):
// - Semicolons added to body statements inside replaced functions (e.g. `const a = 1;` vs `const a = 1`)
// - Space before parens in zero-parameter function expressions (`function ()` vs `function()`)
// - Trailing semicolons on replacement statements (`});` vs `})`)

// The diagnostic should trigger for these cases. We should check that
// the quick fixes have kept the generic type arguments.

export const arrowWithTypeParams = <T>(value: T) => {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(value)
  })
}

export const functionExpressionWithTypeParams = function<T>(value: T) {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(value)
  })
}

export function functionDeclarationWithTypeParams<T>(value: T) {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(value)
  })
}

// Regular functions with type parameters returning Effect (no Effect.gen)
// These have >5 statements so they should trigger the diagnostic

export const arrowWithTypeParamsNoGen = <T>(value: T) => {
  const _a = 1
  const _b = 2
  const _c = 3
  const _d = 4
  const _e = 5
  return Effect.succeed(value)
}

export const functionExpressionWithTypeParamsNoGen = Effect.fn(function <T>(value: T) {
    const _a = 1;
    const _b = 2;
    const _c = 3;
    const _d = 4;
    const _e = 5;
    return Effect.succeed(value);
});

export function functionDeclarationWithTypeParamsNoGen<T>(value: T) {
  const _a = 1
  const _b = 2
  const _c = 3
  const _d = 4
  const _e = 5
  return Effect.succeed(value)
}


=== [D13] Fix 3: "Convert to Effect.fn(\"functionExpressionWithTypeParamsNoGen\")" ===

--- file:///.src/effectFnOpportunity_typeParams.ts ---
import * as Effect from "effect/Effect"

// Note: Known formatting divergences from upstream (TypeScript-Go printer artifacts):
// - Semicolons added to body statements inside replaced functions (e.g. `const a = 1;` vs `const a = 1`)
// - Space before parens in zero-parameter function expressions (`function ()` vs `function()`)
// - Trailing semicolons on replacement statements (`});` vs `})`)

// The diagnostic should trigger for these cases. We should check that
// the quick fixes have kept the generic type arguments.

export const arrowWithTypeParams = <T>(value: T) => {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(value)
  })
}

export const functionExpressionWithTypeParams = function<T>(value: T) {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(value)
  })
}

export function functionDeclarationWithTypeParams<T>(value: T) {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(value)
  })
}

// Regular functions with type parameters returning Effect (no Effect.gen)
// These have >5 statements so they should trigger the diagnostic

export const arrowWithTypeParamsNoGen = <T>(value: T) => {
  const _a = 1
  const _b = 2
  const _c = 3
  const _d = 4
  const _e = 5
  return Effect.succeed(value)
}

export const functionExpressionWithTypeParamsNoGen = Effect.fn("functionExpressionWithTypeParamsNoGen")(function <T>(value: T) {
    const _a = 1;
    const _b = 2;
    const _c = 3;
    const _d = 4;
    const _e = 5;
    return Effect.succeed(value);
});

export function functionDeclarationWithTypeParamsNoGen<T>(value: T) {
  const _a = 1
  const _b = 2
  const _c = 3
  const _d = 4
  const _e = 5
  return Effect.succeed(value)
}


=== [D19] Fix 0: "Disable effectFnOpportunity for this line" ===

=== [D19] Fix 1: "Disable effectFnOpportunity for entire file" ===

=== [D19] Fix 2: "Convert to Effect.fn (no span)" ===

--- file:///.src/effectFnOpportunity_typeParams.ts ---
import * as Effect from "effect/Effect"

// Note: Known formatting divergences from upstream (TypeScript-Go printer artifacts):
// - Semicolons added to body statements inside replaced functions (e.g. `const a = 1;` vs `const a = 1`)
// - Space before parens in zero-parameter function expressions (`function ()` vs `function()`)
// - Trailing semicolons on replacement statements (`});` vs `})`)

// The diagnostic should trigger for these cases. We should check that
// the quick fixes have kept the generic type arguments.

export const arrowWithTypeParams = <T>(value: T) => {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(value)
  })
}

export const functionExpressionWithTypeParams = function<T>(value: T) {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(value)
  })
}

export function functionDeclarationWithTypeParams<T>(value: T) {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(value)
  })
}

// Regular functions with type parameters returning Effect (no Effect.gen)
// These have >5 statements so they should trigger the diagnostic

export const arrowWithTypeParamsNoGen = <T>(value: T) => {
  const _a = 1
  const _b = 2
  const _c = 3
  const _d = 4
  const _e = 5
  return Effect.succeed(value)
}

export const functionExpressionWithTypeParamsNoGen = function<T>(value: T) {
  const _a = 1
  const _b = 2
  const _c = 3
  const _d = 4
  const _e = 5
  return Effect.succeed(value)
}

export const functionDeclarationWithTypeParamsNoGen = Effect.fn(function <T>(value: T) {
    const _a = 1;
    const _b = 2;
    const _c = 3;
    const _d = 4;
    const _e = 5;
    return Effect.succeed(value);
});


=== [D19] Fix 3: "Convert to Effect.fn(\"functionDeclarationWithTypeParamsNoGen\")" ===

--- file:///.src/effectFnOpportunity_typeParams.ts ---
import * as Effect from "effect/Effect"

// Note: Known formatting divergences from upstream (TypeScript-Go printer artifacts):
// - Semicolons added to body statements inside replaced functions (e.g. `const a = 1;` vs `const a = 1`)
// - Space before parens in zero-parameter function expressions (`function ()` vs `function()`)
// - Trailing semicolons on replacement statements (`});` vs `})`)

// The diagnostic should trigger for these cases. We should check that
// the quick fixes have kept the generic type arguments.

export const arrowWithTypeParams = <T>(value: T) => {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(value)
  })
}

export const functionExpressionWithTypeParams = function<T>(value: T) {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(value)
  })
}

export function functionDeclarationWithTypeParams<T>(value: T) {
  return Effect.gen(function*() {
    return yield* Effect.succeed(value)
  })
}

// Regular functions with type parameters returning Effect (no Effect.gen)
// These have >5 statements so they should trigger the diagnostic

export const arrowWithTypeParamsNoGen = <T>(value: T) => {
  const _a = 1
  const _b = 2
  const _c = 3
  const _d = 4
  const _e = 5
  return Effect.succeed(value)
}

export const functionExpressionWithTypeParamsNoGen = function<T>(value: T) {
  const _a = 1
  const _b = 2
  const _c = 3
  const _d = 4
  const _e = 5
  return Effect.succeed(value)
}

export const functionDeclarationWithTypeParamsNoGen = Effect.fn("functionDeclarationWithTypeParamsNoGen")(function <T>(value: T) {
    const _a = 1;
    const _b = 2;
    const _c = 3;
    const _d = 4;
    const _e = 5;
    return Effect.succeed(value);
});
